title: hieracium bupleuroides subsp. calathodiiforme
intro: Hieracium bupleuroides subsp. calathodiiforme is a lesser-known species of hawkweed, a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is a herbaceous perennial plant with medicinal properties, primarily due to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, Hieracium bupleuroides subsp. calathodiiforme is often dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
